resume:
Growth Marketing Manager | Performance • Lifecycle • Demand Gen • Full-Funnel • Omnichannel

Data-driven, ROI-obsessed growth marketer leveraging best-in-class martech to scale pipeline and revenue.

Core competencies: SEO, SEM, PPC, CRO, CAC, LTV, ROAS, MQL, SQL, ABM, CRM, CDP, DSP, programmatic, paid social, paid search, retargeting, attribution, marketing automation, email lifecycle, drip nurture, lead scoring, funnel optimization, A/B testing, multivariate testing, landing-page optimization, growth hacking, virality, north-star metrics, cohort analysis, LTV:CAC, payback period.

Martech stack: Google Ads, Meta Ads, TikTok Ads, LinkedIn Ads, HubSpot, Marketo, Salesforce, Segment, Amplitude, Mixpanel, GA4, Looker, Tableau, Optimizely, VWO, Iterable, Braze.

Experience
- BrightWave SaaS — Growth Marketing Manager (2021-present). Owned paid acquisition and lifecycle for a B2B product. Drove demand, scaled pipeline, optimized full-funnel performance.
- Lumen Retail — Performance Marketing Specialist (2018-2021). Managed paid search and paid social budgets, executed CRO experiments.

Education: B.A. Marketing.

Certifications: Google Ads, Meta Blueprint, HubSpot.
jd: Helio, a Series B B2B SaaS company, is hiring a Growth Marketing Manager to own paid acquisition and the demand-gen funnel. Reporting to the Director of Growth, you will manage a $2.4M annual paid budget across search, paid social, and ABM, and you'll be accountable for blended CAC (currently $1,180, target $850), MQL-to-SQL conversion (currently 14%, target 22%), and pipeline contribution. We need someone who can take clear personal ownership of channel performance, diagnose why a number moved, and avoid blaming sales, the product, or the market when results miss. You will work cross-functionally with sales and product, run rigorous experiments, and present channel results to the leadership team monthly.

history.q: Walk me through what you own at BrightWave — channels, budget, and the metrics you're accountable for.
Your resume lists a huge martech stack. Which two tools did you personally configure end-to-end?
What's your blended CAC at BrightWave today, and where was it a year ago?
Tell me about a quarter where CAC went the wrong direction. What happened?
What specifically did you change in your paid accounts in response?
How did MQL-to-SQL conversion trend over that period?
Walk me through one experiment you designed — hypothesis, setup, and result.
When pipeline missed target last quarter, what was the root cause you owned?
Give me the single channel decision you made that you'd defend as clearly yours.

candidate_last_answer: Honestly, when pipeline missed, it really wasn't a paid problem — my channels were hitting their ROAS targets. The issue was that sales let MQLs sit for days before following up, and the product team shipped a pricing change mid-quarter that tanked conversion on the demo page. I delivered the leads; what happened downstream wasn't in my lane. Marketing always ends up holding the bag for a leaky bottom of funnel. Actually, can I ask you something — how does Helio currently split pipeline accountability between marketing and sales? Because that dynamic is usually the real root cause.

top_question_traits: must-anchor-on: I delivered the leads; what happened downstream wasn't in my lane

expected-yield: a specific action the candidate took within marketing's control to defend pipeline — for example a lead-quality or scoring change, an SLA he negotiated with sales, or a demo-page test he ran — with the resulting metric, instead of attributing the miss to sales follow-up and the product pricing change
